forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system east of Tasmania will maintain a ridge over SA this week. A cold front is expected to weaken and slide across western and southern coasts during Tuesday and Wednesday. A second cold front may pass over the south again on Saturday with a high pressure system to follow over waters south of the Bight on Sunday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches in the south. Dry apart from isolated showers and a possible thunderstorm in the far southwest late afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures above average in the west and south, but near average in the northeast. Northeast to northwesterly winds, fresh at times in the west, ahead of a southerly change in the far southwest later. East to southeasterly winds in the far northeast.
Fine. Mostly clear. Southeasterly winds tending northeasterly in the west.

Isolated light showers about southern coastal districts, mostly during the morning and early afternoon. Maximum temperatures above average. East to southeasterly winds in the far northeast, otherwise northeast to northwesterly winds ahead of a south to southeasterly change extending across the south.

text.p: Dry. Morning fog patches about eastern districts. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northerly winds, but east to southeasterly in the far northeast.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: up to 2 mm for the agricultural area and West Coast district, locally reaching 2 to 5 mm near southern coasts and ranges and the South East districts. Isolated falls of 5 to 10 mm are possible about the Lower South East.
Dry. Cloud increasing across the west and south. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northerly winds, freshening about the west and south during the day.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: up to 2 mm about southern and western coastal areas, mostly occurring on Wednesday.
